payload.title: Strict binary crates can't build from a clean checkout (no wrapper bootstrap)
payload.description:
trust, trust-rustc, and xtask are #![strict]-dogfooded and use named-arg syntax, so 'cargo build -p trust-rustc' under stock rustc FAILS (rustc rejects f(name: val)). But trust-rustc IS the RUSTC_WRAPPER needed to build them — chicken-and-egg. Local dev works only because a prebuilt target/debug/trust-rustc exists; a clean checkout (and CI, once RT-57 stands it up) has none. CI's 'cargo test --workspace' and the fixture steps assume these build plainly.

Fix options: (a) a bootstrap step that builds trust-rustc from a positional-lowered copy first, then self-hosts; (b) commit a pre-lowered trust-rustc; (c) keep trust-rustc/xtask main.rs free of syntax extensions (lints-only) so they build under stock rustc. Tie into RT-57 (Public repo + CI).
